ActionItems: Keep essential expenses under the $11,900 guaranteed-income floor so portfolio withdrawals stay optional.
Revisit the $2,100 average monthly withdrawal at the next Desert Ridge Wealth Partners quarterly review.
Continue directing surplus to the travel fund and the grandkids' college contributions.
BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Monthly household cash-flow and break-even review for Paul and Judith Rivera. Both retired; no earned income and no debt (house and both vehicles paid off). Purpose: confirm guaranteed retirement income covers essential living costs without drawing down the investment portfolio.

CurrentState.Note: Guaranteed income ($11,900) alone covers total monthly expenses ($7,000) with $4,900 to spare; the $2,100 average investment withdrawal is discretionary and flows to the travel fund and the grandkids' college contributions.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Paul is structurally break-even-positive: fixed retirement income ($11,900/mo) exceeds essential expenses by a wide margin, so the portfolio is a growth and legacy asset rather than a spending necessity. The plan holds even if markets require pausing withdrawals.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per Iverson)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Iverson said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.
